q: Heat is the form of energy that can be transferred from one system to another as a result of ___.
___ deals with equilibrium states by outlining the relationship between energy and work.
Heat transfer concerns the generation, use, conversion, and exchange of ___ between physical systems.
One of the basic requirement for heat transfer is the presence of a ___ .
There are three basic ways of heat transfer; namely, heat conduction, heat convection and ___ .
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by ___ , in which the motion of the fluid itself carries heat from one place to another.
In solid, heat is transferred by ___ process in which heat energy is transferred among the adjacent parts of the material without the motion of the material itself.
Heat radiation occurs in the form of ___ waves.
The individual parts of a medium do not move as a whole in ___ .
In heat conduction, heat energy is transferred by ___ distribution among the adjacent parts.
Metals have many ___ , which can carry heat.
The rate of heat conduction through a material of cross-sectional area $A$ is proportional to the ___ $\Delta T$.
The rate of heat conduction is inversely proportional to the ___ $l$ of the material.
___ $k$ is a measure of the ability of a material to conduct heat.
In SI units, $k$ is ___ ($W m^{-1} K^{-1}$).
The ratio $\frac{T_2-T_1}{l}$ is called ___ .
The rate of heat flow per unit area is proportional to the magnitude of ___ .
The mathematical model of heat conduction was first formulated by ___ .
The rate of heat flow per unit area is also known as ___ .
Negative sign in Fourier's law indicates that temperature $T$ must decrease as distance $x$ ___ .
In the SI units, heat current $H$ is ___ ($W$).
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a good ___ .
A low value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a poor thermal conductor or ___ .
___ such as rubber, wood, and styrofoam are poor conductors of heat.
___ is the flow of heat through a fluid from places of higher temperature to places of lower temperature by movement of the fluid itself.
Convection currents are set up in the fluid because the hotter part of the fluid is not as ___ as the cooler part.
Convection occurs only in a liquid or a gas, never in a ___ .
___ force is what drives natural convection.
The basic equation for the rate of convection heat transfer is known as ___ .
In the equation for convection, $q$ is the ___ coefficient.
In SI units, $q$ is expressed ___ ($W m^{-2} K^{-1}$).
c: watt per metre squared per kelvin

q: Some of the weather conditions are created by ___ .
___ is a transfer of heat that does not require a material medium.
Energy transfer by radiation is the fastest (at the speed of light) and it suffers no ___ in a vacuum.
All bodies at a temperature above ___ emit thermal radiation.
Thermal radiation differs from other forms of electromagnetic radiation such as ___ that are not related to temperature.
A ___ object is a good absorber and a good radiator whose emissivity is close to 1.
A white, clear, or silver coated object is a poor absorber and a poor radiator whose ___ is much less than 1.
The emissivity of an object depends on the ___ of radiation.
The object which can absorb all the electromagnetic radiations falling upon it is called a ___ .
